tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows hypoglycemia (low glucose), low HbA1c, and elevated creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), indicating possible nephropathy and metabolic derangement. No evidence of urinary infection. Findings warrant further evaluation for underlying causes such as diabetes with renal involvement, and close monitoring of renal and metabolic status is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
